title: The most famous curve in biochemistry
author: OpenDiscover
blurb: Add more substrate and an enzyme speeds up — but only so far. The Michaelis–Menten equation v = Vmax·[S]/(Km+[S]) bends from a near-linear rise into a flat ceiling as every active site saturates. Km (here 4) is the substrate level giving half of Vmax — a direct read-out of the enzyme's affinity: low Km, tight binding. It is the single most-taught relationship in all of biochemistry, and the reason drug and enzyme assays are run the way they are.
